question_text: A skydiver is falling through the air at terminal velocity (constant speed). Which of the following correctly identifies all the forces and explains why velocity is constant?

options.label: A
options.text: Only weight acts downward; air resistance is negligible, so the skydiver accelerates downward

options.label: B
options.text: Weight (non-contact, downward) and air resistance (contact, upward) are equal in size, making the resultant force zero

options.label: C
options.text: Air resistance is greater than weight, pushing the skydiver back upward

options.label: D
options.text: Weight and air resistance are a Newton's third law pair in equilibrium

explanation: At terminal velocity, the downward weight force equals the upward air resistance force (both contact and non-contact examples). The resultant force is zero, so there is no acceleration and velocity stays constant. They're not a third law pair because both act on the skydiver.
